title: overlayfs daemon left after container is finished
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug


**Description**
There are multiple overlayfs daemons left when the containers are finished. The daemons are cleaned until manually remove the containers. 


<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. There is no overlay daemon
```
[lsfadmin@rhel8x-mgmt-2 log]$ ps -ef | grep overlay
lsfadmin  518288  212974  0 21:56 pts/3    00:00:00 grep --color=auto overlay
```
2. run a container and make it done
```
[lsfadmin@rhel8x-mgmt-2 log]$ podman run ubuntu id
uid=0(root) gid=0(root) groups=0(root)
[lsfadmin@rhel8x-mgmt-2 log]$ ps -ef | grep overlay
lsfadmin  518385       1  0 21:56 ?        00:00:00 /usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs -o metacopy=on,lowerdir=/podmanfs/lsfadmin/storage/overlay/l/JX275L6OJLN3U4OW2CVUU44DKI,upperdir=/podmanfs/lsfadmin/storage/overlay/68a290a3a77bcc194f66143fd7842dab3cb170ee3a35c459717b2e55105d4ec6/diff,workdir=/podmanfs/lsfadmin/storage/overlay/68a290a3a77bcc194f66143fd7842dab3cb170ee3a35c459717b2e55105d4ec6/work /podmanfs/lsfadmin/storage/overlay/68a290a3a77bcc194f66143fd7842dab3cb170ee3a35c459717b2e55105d4ec6/merged
lsfadmin  518466  212974  0 21:56 pts/3    00:00:00 grep --color=auto overlay
```
3. more containers more daemon leaks
```
[lsfadmin@rhel8x-mgmt-2 log]$ podman run ubuntu id
uid=0(root) gid=0(root) groups=0(root)
[lsfadmin@rhel8x-mgmt-2 log]$ ps -ef | grep overlay
lsfadmin  518385       1  0 21:56 ?        00:00:00 /usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs -o metacopy=on,lowerdir=/podmanfs/lsfadmin/storage/overlay/l/JX275L6OJLN3U4OW2CVUU44DKI,upperdir=/podmanfs/lsfadmin/storage/overlay/68a290a3a77bcc194f66143fd7842dab3cb170ee3a35c459717b2e55105d4ec6/diff,workdir=/podmanfs/lsfadmin/storage/overlay/68a290a3a77bcc194f66143fd7842dab3cb170ee3a35c459717b2e55105d4ec6/work /podmanfs/lsfadmin/storage/overlay/68a290a3a77bcc194f66143fd7842dab3cb170ee3a35c459717b2e55105d4ec6/merged
lsfadmin  519470       1  0 22:06 ?        00:00:00 /usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs -o metacopy=on,lowerdir=/podmanfs/lsfadmin/storage/overlay/l/JX275L6OJLN3U4OW2CVUU44DKI,upperdir=/podmanfs/lsfadmin/storage/overlay/8f304342bfadb261258006f715d23fd8262f622e129d1e4b6d9b9cf8ae0b4715/diff,workdir=/podmanfs/lsfadmin/storage/overlay/8f304342bfadb261258006f715d23fd8262f622e129d1e4b6d9b9cf8ae0b4715/work /podmanfs/lsfadmin/storage/overlay/8f304342bfadb261258006f715d23fd8262f622e129d1e4b6d9b9cf8ae0b4715/merged
lsfadmin  519557  212974  0 22:06 pts/3    00:00:00 grep --color=auto overlay

[lsfadmin@rhel8x-mgmt-2 log]$ podman run ubuntu id
uid=0(root) gid=0(root) groups=0(root)
[lsfadmin@rhel8x-mgmt-2 log]$ ps -ef | grep overlay
lsfadmin  518385       1  0 21:56 ?        00:00:00 /usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs -o metacopy=on,lowerdir=/podmanfs/lsfadmin/storage/overlay/l/JX275L6OJLN3U4OW2CVUU44DKI,upperdir=/podmanfs/lsfadmin/storage/overlay/68a290a3a77bcc194f66143fd7842dab3cb170ee3a35c459717b2e55105d4ec6/diff,workdir=/podmanfs/lsfadmin/storage/overlay/68a290a3a77bcc194f66143fd7842dab3cb170ee3a35c459717b2e55105d4ec6/work /podmanfs/lsfadmin/storage/overlay/68a290a3a77bcc194f66143fd7842dab3cb170ee3a35c459717b2e55105d4ec6/merged
lsfadmin  519470       1  0 22:06 ?        00:00:00 /usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs -o metacopy=on,lowerdir=/podmanfs/lsfadmin/storage/overlay/l/JX275L6OJLN3U4OW2CVUU44DKI,upperdir=/podmanfs/lsfadmin/storage/overlay/8f304342bfadb261258006f715d23fd8262f622e129d1e4b6d9b9cf8ae0b4715/diff,workdir=/podmanfs/lsfadmin/storage/overlay/8f304342bfadb261258006f715d23fd8262f622e129d1e4b6d9b9cf8ae0b4715/work /podmanfs/lsfadmin/storage/overlay/8f304342bfadb261258006f715d23fd8262f622e129d1e4b6d9b9cf8ae0b4715/merged
lsfadmin  519647       1  0 22:06 ?        00:00:00 /usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs -o metacopy=on,lowerdir=/podmanfs/lsfadmin/storage/overlay/l/JX275L6OJLN3U4OW2CVUU44DKI,upperdir=/podmanfs/lsfadmin/storage/overlay/26cd2e7303b0855ee3d76e7a450017844338387e6c3936580da2e91f674747b0/diff,workdir=/podmanfs/lsfadmin/storage/overlay/26cd2e7303b0855ee3d76e7a450017844338387e6c3936580da2e91f674747b0/work /podmanfs/lsfadmin/storage/overlay/26cd2e7303b0855ee3d76e7a450017844338387e6c3936580da2e91f674747b0/merged
lsfadmin  519725  212974  0 22:06 pts/3    00:00:00 grep --color=auto overlay
```

4. after remove finished containers, overlay daemons are cleaned
```
[lsfadmin@rhel8x-mgmt-2 log]$ podman ps 
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE       COMMAND     CREATED     STATUS      PORTS       NAMES
[lsfadmin@rhel8x-mgmt-2 log]$ podman ps -qa
dca8e8c28102
1a21c1ebaf30
156e0101a7d1
[lsfadmin@rhel8x-mgmt-2 log]$ podman ps -qa | xargs podman rm
156e0101a7d18250d8df9d83e309f504bffa1c4cfb56142584a1f0eecfbe79de
dca8e8c28102540751106b4d28ce8a21685c75c7dd4037fc6e7c656cef58bec9
1a21c1ebaf3084c601a88f177fb3c64e927a0a688e5de974103c3b6cb599fac5
[lsfadmin@rhel8x-mgmt-2 log]$ ps -ef | grep overlay
lsfadmin  520266  212974  0 22:08 pts/3    00:00:00 grep --color=auto overlay
```
**Describe the results you received:**
when a container is done, overlay daemon is left and accumulated when more and more containers are done.

**Describe the results you expected:**
when a container is done, all running daemons of a container should be exited.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.4.2
API Version:  3.4.2
Go Version:   go1.16.7
Built:        Thu Jan 13 05:15:49 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
[lsfadmin@rhel8x-mgmt-2 log]$ rpm -q podman
podman-3.4.2-9.module+el8.5.0+13852+150547f7.x86_64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes/No

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: This is not observed on main branch, 4.0.2
could have been some failure when cleaning up the container, could you try running manually `podman container cleanup $CONTAINER_ID`?  Does it show any error message?
Since you are running on RHEL.  Please report issues via the Red Hat support channels (e.g., Bugzilla).  This way, we can properly prioritize the issue and prepare backups if needed.

with `podman container cleanaup`, the overlay process is finished. There is no error message showed.

```
[lsfadmin@rhel8x-mgmt-2 log]$ podman ps
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE       COMMAND     CREATED     STATUS      PORTS       NAMES
[lsfadmin@rhel8x-mgmt-2 log]$ ps -ef | grep podman
lsfadmin   95142       1  0 Mar16 ?        00:00:00 podman
lsfadmin  891551  212974  0 21:08 pts/3    00:00:00 grep --color=auto podman
[lsfadmin@rhel8x-mgmt-2 log]$ ps -ef | grep overlay
lsfadmin  891559  212974  0 21:08 pts/3    00:00:00 grep --color=auto overlay


[lsfadmin@rhel8x-mgmt-2 log]$ podman run ubuntu id
uid=0(root) gid=0(root) groups=0(root)
[lsfadmin@rhel8x-mgmt-2 log]$ ps -ef | grep overlay
lsfadmin  891649       1  0 21:08 ?        00:00:00 /usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs -o metacopy=on,lowerdir=/podmanfs/lsfadmin/storage/overlay/l/JX275L6OJLN3U4OW2CVUU44DKI,upperdir=/podmanfs/lsfadmin/storage/overlay/c03f692375dac4139da34a566092528bea5cbc1aecb478ee8565b8337eeca4c8/diff,workdir=/podmanfs/lsfadmin/storage/overlay/c03f692375dac4139da34a566092528bea5cbc1aecb478ee8565b8337eeca4c8/work /podmanfs/lsfadmin/storage/overlay/c03f692375dac4139da34a566092528bea5cbc1aecb478ee8565b8337eeca4c8/merged
lsfadmin  891730  212974  0 21:08 pts/3    00:00:00 grep --color=auto overlay
[lsfadmin@rhel8x-mgmt-2 log]$ podman ps -a
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                            COMMAND     CREATED        STATUS                    PORTS       NAMES
2b39a6bc9739  docker.io/library/ubuntu:latest  id          5 seconds ago  Exited (0) 5 seconds ago              reverent_bhabha

[lsfadmin@rhel8x-mgmt-2 log]$ podman container  cleanup 2b39a6bc9739
2b39a6bc9739d89677e0340d440a274862a4d3b65962c2f859b436cc40f9dc65
[lsfadmin@rhel8x-mgmt-2 log]$ ps -ef | grep overlay
lsfadmin  891943  212974  0 21:09 pts/3    00:00:00 grep --color=auto overlay
[lsfadmin@rhel8x-mgmt-2 log]$ podman ps -a
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                            COMMAND     CREATED         STATUS                     PORTS       NAMES
2b39a6bc9739  docker.io/library/ubuntu:latest  id          40 seconds ago  Exited (0) 40 seconds ago              reverent_bhabha
```

I have 227 of these:

```
$ ps -ef | grep overlay | wc -l
227
```

I have no podman containers running at all:

```
$ podman ps -a
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE       COMMAND     CREATED     STATUS      PORTS       NAMES
```

```
$ podman --version
podman version 3.4.4
```

on Fedora 35.

What should I do to clean up all of these orphans?  Just kill their processes?  Will that leave behind any other garbage that I need to manually collect?

Any idea how they are being created?

ps -eZ | grep podman

```
$ ps -eZ | grep podman
unconfined_u:system_r:container_runtime_t:s0-s0:c0.c1023 932059 ? 00:00:00 podman pause
```

As for how they are being created, I'm not sure how to answer that any other way than to say because I have been running podman, but that seems like too simple an answer.  Maybe I am not understanding your question.

If you just run another container 

$ podman run --rm fedora echo hi

Does the number of fuse-overlay mounts grow?

How about if you run without the --rm?

$ podman run fedora echo hi

> If you just run another container
> 
> $ podman run --rm fedora echo hi
> 
> Does the number of fuse-overlay mounts grow?

No.

> How about if you run without the --rm?
> 
> $ podman run fedora echo hi

No.

Still at 227 after both operations above.

Try with failure codes

$ podman run fedora exit -1

BTW You should be able to kill all of those fuse-overalays
$ killall -9 fuse-overlayfs

> Try with failure codes
> 
> $ podman run fedora exit -1

Modified:

```
podman run fedora bash -c "exit -1"
```

> BTW You should be able to kill all of those fuse-overalays $ killall -9 fuse-overlayfs

I am probably fine to just do that for now and see if this problem reproduces, now that I know to watch out for it.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Since you have not reported any new ones, I am going to assume this is fixed and close.  Reopen if it happens again.
